alloy_matches.reason: Identical alloy names, base element, alloying elements, and nominal composition.

process_matches.reason: Same process description with minor added detail in prediction; linked to same alloy and sample.

sample_matches.reason: Same sample ID, linked to matching alloy and process, and consistent context.

truth.papers.A57.title: An Ultrastrong and Ductile Duplex Lightweight Steel with Dual-Nanoprecipitation
truth.papers.A57.paper_scope: Investigation of microstructure and mechanical properties of Fe-30Mn-9Al-1C-1V-5Ni duplex lightweight steel with dual-nanoprecipitation (L'12 ordered domains and VC carbides) produced by cold rolling and heat treatment, focusing on strengthening and strain hardening mechanisms.

prediction.papers.A57.title: An Ultrastrong and Ductile Duplex Lightweight Steel with Dual-Nanoprecipitation
prediction.papers.A57.paper_scope: Design and investigation of an Fe-30Mn-9Al-1C-1V-5Ni duplex lightweight steel with austenite-B2 dual-phase microstructure and dual-nanoprecipitation (L'1_2 LRO domains and VC) via simple rolling and heat treatment, achieving ultrahigh strength and ductility, and elucidation of multiple strengthening and strain hardening mechanisms.

truth.alloys.alloy_fe30mn9al1c1v5ni.alloys_not: Measured composition (wt.%, Table 1): Mn 30.45, Al 9.50, C 1.20, V 1.09, Ni 4.94, P 0.0040, S 0.0044, Fe Bal.

prediction.alloys.alloy_fe30mn9al1c1v5ni.alloys_not: Measured compositions (wt.%): Mn 30.45, Al 9.50, C 1.20, V 1.09, Ni 4.94, P 0.0040, S 0.0044, Fe Bal.

truth.processes.proc_fe30mn9al1c1v5ni_01.descr: Homogenized at 1050°C for 1 h, hot-rolled to 3 mm (85% reduction), water-quenched, cold-rolled to 1.5 mm (50% total reduction, single pass ≤5%), solution-treated at 900°C for 15 min, water-quenched.

prediction.processes.proc_fe30mn9al1c1v5ni_01.descr: Induction melting of 10 kg ingot under high-purity argon, homogenization at 1050°C for 1 h, hot rolling to 3 mm (~85% reduction), water quenching, cold rolling to 1.5 mm (50% total reduction, single-pass ≤5%), solution treatment at 900°C for 15 min, and final water quenching.

truth.processing_steps.sample_fe30mn9al1c1v5ni: Cold rolled to 1.5 mm final thickness

truth.structures.sample_fe30mn9al1c1v5ni_01.ov: Duplex microstructure of austenite (γ-FCC) and B2 (ordered BCC) phases, with nanoscale L'12 ordered domains (average size 1.0±0.3 nm, volume fraction 11.10%) and VC carbides (average size 19.9±6.2 nm, volume fraction 0.26%) within the austenite.
truth.structures.sample_fe30mn9al1c1v5ni_01.mi: Austenite ~80%, B2 20%, VC 0.26%, LRO domains 11.10% (within austenite)
long strip-like large grains and fine particles decorating austenite grain boundaries

prediction.structures.sample_fe30mn9al1c1v5ni_01.ov: Duplex microstructure comprising austenite and B2 phases with dual-nanoprecipitation of L'1_2 type long-range ordered domains and VC carbides within austenite
prediction.structures.sample_fe30mn9al1c1v5ni_01.mi: long strip-like large grains and fine particles decorating austenite grain boundaries
Residual dislocations from incomplete annihilation during 900°C annealing; initial GND density ~5.36×10^13 m^-2 in austenite and ~1.04×10^14 m^-2 in B2
Austenite shows unimodal distribution; B2 exhibits bimodal distribution with mean sizes of 1.02 µm and 3.38 µm

truth.interfaces.sample_fe30mn9al1c1v5ni_01.de: during deformation, GNDs accumulate at interfaces causing HDI strengthening
truth.interfaces.sample_fe30mn9al1c1v5ni_01.ph: During tensile deformation, dislocations shear the LRO domains reducing their volume fraction; VC precipitates impede dislocation motion; dislocation density increases in both phases and accumulates at phase boundaries, enhancing HDI hardening.

prediction.interfaces.sample_fe30mn9al1c1v5ni_01.de: dislocation pile-up and GND accumulation at heterogeneous interfaces

Accumulation of geometrically necessary dislocations at austenite/B2 boundaries generates long-range back stresses in soft regions and forward stresses in hard regions, producing HDI strengthening and work-hardening
prediction.interfaces.sample_fe30mn9al1c1v5ni_01.in: Rapid relaxation of internal stress and strain at austenite/B2 interface after yielding of hard B2 phase; pronounced strain gradients develop during deformation due to strain incompatibility between phases and grains
Austenite/B2 interface impedes planar dislocation slip and reflects incoming dislocations, leading to formation of new planar dislocation arrays in the austenitic matrix near phase boundaries
prediction.interfaces.sample_fe30mn9al1c1v5ni_01.ph: During deformation, dislocations shear through L'1_2 LRO domains causing partial destruction and reduced volume fraction; VC particles impede dislocation motion with hindering effect becoming more pronounced at higher strains; dislocation density increases significantly in both austenite and B2 phases with straining

truth.properties.sample_fe30mn9al1c1v5ni_01.me: Engineering stress-strain curve shown in Figure 4a; true stress-strain and work hardening rate curves shown in Figure 4b

truth.characterization_methods.sample_fe30mn9a: Evaluate HDI stress evolution and Bauschinger effect
HDI stress increases with strain (from ~620 MPa at 1% to ~845 MPa at 10.4%); yield drop Δσ_y and hysteresis loops reflect HDI strengthening; at yielding, HDI stress extrapolated to 597 MPa

prediction.characterization_methods.sample_fe30mn9a: HDI stress evaluation and Bauschinger effect analysis

Hysteresis area and yield drop increase with strain; HDI stress increases progressively from ~620 MPa at 1% true strain to ~845 MPa at 11% true strain; HDI contribution to YS determined as 597 MPa by extrapolation

truth.computational_details.sample_fe30mn9al1c: Thermodynamic calculations using CALPHAD and analytical strengthening models
Equilibrium phase diagram predictions consistent with XRD and EBSD phase fractions; calculated yield strength matches experimental 1316 MPa
σ_YS = σ_γ f_γ + σ_B2 f_B2 + σ_HDI (Equation 1)
based on measured composition (wt.%): C 1.20, Mn 30.45, Al 9.50, Ni 4.94, V 1.09; σ_ss,γ = 228 MPa
σ_VC = 0.538 G b f_VC^(1/2) / D_VC ln(D_VC / 2b) (Equation 6)
G=70 GPa, b=0.260 nm, f_VC=0.0026, D_VC=19.9 nm → σ_VC = 92 MPa
yield HDI stress = 597 MPa (extrapolated from Figure 5d)
derived from difference after accounting for other mechanisms

prediction.computational_details.idx_0.simulation_m: Thermodynamic equilibrium calculation and analytical strengthening modeling
prediction.computational_details.idx_0.validation_a: Equilibrium phase diagram predictions consistent with XRD and EBSD observed phase fractions; analytical model calculations of yield strength compared against experimental YS
prediction.computational_details.idx_0.analytical_m: Overall YS from rule of mixtures with HDI term
σ_YS = σ_γ f_γ + σ_B2 f_B2 + σ_HDI
σ_HDI obtained by extrapolation from LUR curve equals 597 MPa
Sum of strengthening contributions in austenite
σ_γ = σ_0,γ + σ_ss,γ + σ_dis,γ + σ_VC,γ + σ_LRO,γ
σ_0,γ = 97 MPa; σ_ss,γ = 228 MPa; σ_dis,γ = 106 MPa; σ_VC,γ = 92 MPa; σ_LRO,γ = 119 MPa by difference
Sum of strengthening contributions in B2 phase
Calculate solid solution strengthening in austenite from composition
Calculated value 228 MPa using mass fractions of alloying elements
Calculated contribution 92 MPa based on VC fraction and average diameter

truth.unmapped_findings.idx_0.value: The fracture surface exhibits dimples, indicating a ductile fracture mode (Figure 4a inset).
truth.unmapped_findings.idx_1.value: The work-hardening curve shows three distinct stages: Stage I (0-1% true strain) sharp decrease with rate dropping below zero; Stage II (1-4.3%) rapid increase; Stage III (4.3-12%) gradual decrease but work-hardening rate remains high at 2.7-3.7 GPa.
truth.unmapped_findings.idx_2.value: LUR tests reveal yield drop Δσ_y and hysteresis loops that enlarge with strain; HDI stress increases progressively from about 620 MPa at 1% strain to about 845 MPa at 10.4% strain.
truth.unmapped_findings.idx_3.value: The measured chemical composition (Table 1) is: Mn 30.45, Al 9.50, C 1.20, V 1.09, Ni 4.94, P 0.0040, S 0.0044, Fe Bal. (wt.%).
truth.unmapped_findings.idx_4.value: GND density evolution from in-situ EBSD: in austenite from 5.36×10^13 to 2.64×10^14 m^-2, in B2 from 1.04×10^14 to 2.51×10^14 m^-2 as strain increases from 0% to 6%.
truth.unmapped_findings.idx_5.value: The volume fraction of LRO domains decreases from 11.10% in the initial state after deformation due to dislocations shearing the ordered domains.
truth.unmapped_findings.idx_6.value: The steel was produced via a simple processing route (homogenization, hot rolling, cold rolling, solution treatment) without complex thermomechanical treatments.
truth.unmapped_findings.idx_7.value: Schmid factor distributions were employed to explain variations in GND densities among individual austenite and B2 grains.
truth.unmapped_findings.idx_8.value: The B2 phase has a bimodal grain size distribution with mean sizes of 1.02 µm and 3.38 µm.

prediction.unmapped_findings.idx_0.value: The equilibrium phase diagram calculated by Thermo-Calc 2021b indicates phase fractions at 900°C of approximately 78% austenite, 20% B2, 1% VC, and 0% κ-carbide.
prediction.unmapped_findings.idx_1.value: The fracture surface of the tensile specimen is characterized by dimples, indicating a ductile fracture mode.
prediction.unmapped_findings.idx_2.value: Work-hardening rate behavior exhibits three discernible stages: stage I (0–1%) sharp decrease to below zero; stage II (1–4.3%) rapid increase to a maximum; stage III (>4.3%) gradual decrease while remaining at a relatively high level of 2.7 to 3.7 GPa.
prediction.unmapped_findings.idx_3.value: LUR test detailed evolution: hysteresis area increases from 70 MPa at 1% true strain to 202 MPa at 11% true strain; yield drop Δσy increases from 2 MPa to 23 MPa over the same range.
prediction.unmapped_findings.idx_4.value: GND density evolution during tensile deformation: austenite increases from 5.36×10^13 m^-2 at 0% strain to 2.64×10^14 m^-2 at 6% strain; B2 increases from 1.04×10^14 m^-2 at 0% strain to 2.51×10^14 m^-2 at 6% strain.
prediction.unmapped_findings.idx_5.value: During deformation, L'1_2 LRO domains within austenite slip bands are sheared by dislocations, leading to weakened superlattice reflections and reduced volume fraction; conversely, VC particles impede dislocation motion and cause dislocation accumulation around them.
prediction.unmapped_findings.idx_6.value: Calculated weighted strengthening contributions summarized in Figure 9: σ_HDI 597 MPa; σ_0,γ f_γ 78 MPa; σ_ss,γ f_γ 182 MPa; σ_dis,γ f_γ 85 MPa; σ_VC f_γ 74 MPa; σ_LRO f_γ 119 MPa; σ_0,B2 f_B2 135 MPa; σ_dis,B2 f_B2 46 MPa.
prediction.unmapped_findings.idx_7.value: The B2 phase exhibits a higher hardness than ferrite, and the hardness mismatch between austenite and B2 phases is regulated by precipitation behavior within austenite to balance strength-ductility synergy.
